plan_description: Scans Gmail Inbox emails, flags urgent messages based on keywords in subject/body, marks them as important, labels them as reviewed, and emails you a summary of what changed.

data: - Search Gmail for emails in the Inbox from the last 30 days (read and unread).
- For each email found, read the email subject text.
- For each email found, read the email body text (plain text or best-available text version).
- Exclude any email that already has the tracking label "AgentsPilot".
actions: - Determine whether an email is urgent by checking whether the subject OR body contains any of these keywords/phrases (case-insensitive): "today", "urgent", "immediately", "now", "sensitive".
- If the email is urgent, mark the email as important using Gmail’s built-in important flag.
- If the email is urgent, apply the Gmail label "AgentsPilot" to the email so it will be skipped in future runs.
- If the email is not urgent, do nothing (leave the email unchanged).
output: - Create a summary list of all emails that were marked important in this run.
- For each email in the summary list, include: sender, subject, received date/time, and a short note of which keyword(s) matched (if determinable).
- If no emails were marked important, the summary must explicitly say that no urgent emails were found.
delivery: - Send the summary as an email to meiribarak@gmail.com.
- Use a clear subject line such as: "Urgent emails marked Important (summary)".
- Put the summary directly in the email body (no attachments).
processing_steps: - Build a Gmail search query that targets Inbox emails and excludes the tracking label "AgentsPilot".
- For each email, evaluate the urgency keyword rules against subject and body.
- For urgent emails, apply the important flag and the "AgentsPilot" label.
- Compile and send the summary email.
